$schema_note: Source of truth for docs/issue-detection-tool-capabilities-matrix.md. Edit THIS file, then run scripts/issue_detection_tool_capabilities_matrix_report.py to regenerate the doc's tables + per-item notes. See that script for the renderer.

scale.meaning: A standard rule/analyzer detects essentially all instances deterministically (mechanical pattern).
A rule catches some special cases / a syntactic proxy, but misses the general semantic form (false negatives), or needs opt-in config.
No off-the-shelf detector; requires semantic/contextual judgement (LLM or human).
The construct does not exist in that language, so there is nothing for the tool to detect — distinct from a blank (construct exists, but no detector).
Not yet researched — this item/tool cell has not been investigated. A research-worklist placeholder, distinct from a determined blank or n/a.

items.name: Validate at boundaries
items.note: Semgrep/CodeQL taint-track *unvalidated input → sink* for known sink shapes (SQL/exec/path); JS SonarJS + PHP PHPStan flag some injection patterns. "Should have run a schema parse" in general is semantic → most languages blank.

items.name: Runtime schema validation
items.note: TS-ESLint {tseslint-unsafe-ma} / {tseslint-no-any} (and Py mypy, PHP PHPStan strict) catch the *symptom* (raw `any`/untyped cast from external) partially; none can know a Zod/schema parse was *expected*. Our planted `as {…}` cast → `no-unsafe-member-access` would warn.

items.name: Error Hiding
items.note: **Empty catch** is mechanical and broadly covered (Py `try/except: pass` via Pylint/Ruff {ruff-s110}, Java PMD {pmd-emptycatch}, C# {sonar-s2486}, JS SonarJS `S2486` / {eslint-no-empty} + Biome {biome-no-empty-block}, PHP, Ruby RuboCop {rubocop-suppressed}). A *logs-nothing-meaningful* swallow that isn't empty drops to ∙ (TS/Go/Rust/C++ here). ✓ Our TS plant was **revised** from `catch (e) {}` to exactly that non-empty swallow so it survives cal.com's lint (now ∙, not ✓). Validation caveat: every one of these rules only fires on a *truly empty* (or comment-only) catch — SonarJS/`no-empty`/Biome/`S110` all ignore a catch that contains a comment, and C# `S2486` only flags catches of the *generic* `Exception`. So even the ✓ languages drop to ∙ for the "logs something useless" form; the ✓ here means "deterministic on the *empty* variant" specifically.

items.name: Fallback semantics (a||b)
items.note: wrong-*actor* fallback is pure semantics; no detector in any language knows an attendee email ≠ organizer email. Fully blank. **Grouping-relevant.**

items.name: Long Method
items.note: Pure metric threshold (method length), mechanical in every ecosystem (ESLint {eslint-max-lines-fn}, SonarJS, PMD, Pylint, Clippy, staticcheck, RuboCop `Metrics/*`). ✓ as a *metric*; the underlying smell ("too long to understand") is a proxy. Marked ∙ for TS only because cal.com's config does not enable these metric rules.

items.name: Long Parameter List
items.note: Pure metric threshold (parameter count), mechanical in every ecosystem (ESLint {eslint-max-params}, SonarJS, PMD, Pylint, Clippy, staticcheck, RuboCop `Metrics/*`). ✓ as a *metric*; the underlying smell is a proxy. Marked ∙ for TS only because cal.com's config does not enable these metric rules.

items.name: Deep Nesting
items.note: Pure metric threshold (nesting depth), mechanical in every ecosystem (ESLint {eslint-max-depth}, SonarJS, PMD, Pylint, Clippy, staticcheck, RuboCop `Metrics/*`). ✓ as a *metric*; the underlying smell is a proxy. Marked ∙ for TS only because cal.com's config does not enable these metric rules.

items.name: Do one thing (fn SRP)
items.note: only *proxied* by complexity/length metrics; "does several unrelated things" is semantic → ∙ where a cognitive-complexity rule exists, blank where it doesn't. **Grouping-relevant.**

items.name: Dead Code
items.note: unused locals/imports/params + unreachable code: complete almost everywhere (Py Pyflakes/Ruff, Java, C# Roslyn, JS `no-unused-vars`, TS `noUnusedLocals`, Go {go-unused} on unused, Rust {rust-dead-code} lint, RuboCop). C/C++ only ∙ (warnings, weaker reachability). **But** dead *across module boundaries* needs whole-program reachability → corpus must plant *cross-boundary* dead code or the repo's own lint flags it. Validation caveat: Go's compile-time unused error is *function-local only* — package-level vars and unexported funcs/methods are **not** compile errors (staticcheck `U1000` catches some), which is exactly why the cross-boundary form is the interesting plant; the ✓ is for the local case.

items.name: Duplicate Code
items.note: token/AST clone detection: Sonar CPD, PMD CPD, `jscpd`, Py pylint `duplicate-code`, RuboCop. ✓ for copy-paste blocks above a token threshold; near-duplicate-with-edits = partial. Blank for TS because cal.com doesn't run a CPD pass in its lint.

items.name: Magic Numbers / Strings
items.note: {eslint-no-magic} (JS), SonarJS `S109`, PMD {pmd-avoid-literals}, Pylint `magic-value`, RuboCop exist but **high false-positive**, usually off; only "unnamed numeric literal" → ∙ everywhere (misses magic *strings*, allows config'd exceptions).

items.name: Reinvent the Wheel
items.note: only narrow cases: `you-dont-need-lodash-underscore` (cal.com runs it!) flags hand-rolled lodash equivalents; Clippy/RuboCop/Ruff flag a few "use the stdlib" idioms. General "a lib already does this" = semantic → mostly blank. **Grouping-relevant.**

items.name: Strict equality (==)
items.note: Loose-equality coercion footgun. Applies to **JS/TS and PHP**, which both have a type-coercing `==`: ESLint {eslint-eqeqeq} / SonarJS / Biome `noDoubleEquals` → ✓ for JS/TS, and PHPStan {phpstan-strict} (+ Psalm) flag it ∙ for PHP. Every other language is `n/a`: the coercion construct doesn't exist there — **Py / Go / Rust / Ruby** have no loose `==`, and in **C / C++ / Java / C#** a literal `==` means value/reference compare, not coercion, so there is no equivalent construct to flag. (Not a JS/TS-only smell — hence the generic "language footgun" family rather than a JS/TS label.)

items.name: Mutable default argument
items.note: Checklist item 17.6.5; not plantable in the cal.com TS corpus (no D-group). **Python-only** footgun: a mutable default (`def f(x=[])`) is shared across calls. Detected ✓ by Ruff {ruff-b006} / Pylint {pylint-w0102}. `n/a` everywhere else — no other language in the matrix evaluates default-argument expressions once at definition time the way Python does.

items.name: this context loss
items.note: Receiver-binding footgun (dynamic `this` rebinding) — **JS/TS-only**: no other language has detached-method `this` rebinding, so every non-JS/TS cell is `n/a` (construct absent). TS-ESLint {tseslint-unbound} / {tseslint-no-this} → ∙ (the rebinding is undecidable).

items.name: var hoisting
items.note: Scoping footgun (function-scoped `var` hoisting) — **JS/TS-only**: no other language has it, so every non-JS/TS cell is `n/a` (construct absent). ESLint {eslint-no-var} / SonarJS / Biome `noVar` → ✓.

items.name: Implicit numeric/narrowing coercion
items.note: Cross-language reference row (not plantable in the cal.com TS corpus). The non-`==` coercion analogue to 17.6.4 in statically-mixed-numeric languages: silent narrowing / implicit conversion. **C / C++** ∙ via clang-tidy {clang-narrowing} or `gcc` {gcc-wconversion}; **Go** ∙ (untyped-const conversions, {staticcheck-sa4017}); **Java** ∙ via SpotBugs {spotbugs-icast}; **PHP** ∙ (PHPStan/Psalm numeric-string coercion). `n/a` for **Py / Rust / JS / TS / C# / Ruby**, whose coercion smell is either the loose-`==` form already in 17.6.4 or is prevented by the type system.

items.name: Nil receiver / interface-nil comparison
items.note: Cross-language reference row (not plantable in the cal.com TS corpus). **Go-only** binding footgun analogous to 17.6.6: a typed-nil stored in an interface is `!= nil`, and a nil pointer receiver still dispatches. ∙ via {govet-nilfunc} / staticcheck (`SA4023` etc.) — partial because the general typed-nil case is undecidable. `n/a` elsewhere: no other language has Go's interface-nil / value-receiver semantics.

items.name: Unsafe nil-chain (safe-navigation misuse)
items.note: Cross-language reference row (not plantable in the cal.com TS corpus). **Ruby-only**: chaining a regular method after a safe-navigation `&.` re-introduces the `nil` crash the `&.` was meant to prevent. ∙ via RuboCop {rubocop-safenav}. `n/a` elsewhere — the `&.` operator (and this specific misuse) is Ruby-specific; JS/TS `?.` short-circuits the whole chain so it lacks the analogous trap.

items.name: Clean — Inner knows nothing of outer
One level of abstraction per function
Fake It Till You Make It
items.short: Fake It Till You Make It

items.name: Explicit over implicit (no request context in models)
Don't assume ownership of shared/global resources

tool_capability_difference_notes: **mypy / `tsc`** are *type-checkers*, not lint rule engines: they catch unused code (`tsc` `noUnusedLocals`) and unsafe-`any` symptoms but have no empty-catch / metric / magic-number / clone rules → blank on those rows.
**cppcheck** has no copy-paste (CPD) clone pass, so 16.4.1 is blank for it while clang-tidy-adjacent tooling can flag some → only clang-tidy carries the C/C++ duplicate ∙.
**SpotBugs** is bug-pattern (bytecode) focused: it lacks a magic-number rule and a CPD clone pass (those live in **PMD** / PMD-CPD), so 16.7.6 and 16.4.1 are PMD-only on the Java side.
**Biome** ships a smaller rule set than ESLint/SonarJS: it has `noEmptyBlockStatements`, `noDoubleEquals`, `noVar` (✓ there) but no metric/magic-number/CPD rules → blank on those.
**golangci-lint** bundles many linters (incl. `gocyclo`/`funlen`/`dupl`) so it carries the metric + SRP coverage that bare **staticcheck** doesn't; both catch the unused/dead-code case.
**PHPStan vs Psalm** track closely for these items; the loose-`==` ban (17.6.4 ∙) is PHPStan strict-rules and Psalm has the analogous `insane-comparison` / strict-equality checks.
